name: Spinach Torta Rustica with Potato "Crust
recipeIngredient: 2 large Baking potatoes, sliced thin (about 1/4 inch slices)
1 tablespoon olive oil plus a little extra
1/4 cup Italian seasoned bread crumbs (divided use)
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
1 can (7 ounces) roasted red peppers, peeled (about 2 peppers if roasting your own)
1/4 lb smoked turkey breast, sliced 1/8-inch thick and 1/2 inch wide strips (or substitute any high-quality smoked ham)
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam potatoes until barely tender, about 7 to 10 minutes. Watch them carefully so they don't get too soft.
2. Cook spinach according to package directions just until completely thawed (or thaw overnight in the refrigerator). Drain spinach in a colander and rinse under water to bring to room temperature. Drain spinach again. Squeeze handfuls of spinach tightly to wring out excess moisture. The spinach should be almost completely dry. Set aside.
3. Preheat oven to 375°F. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a small skillet. Saute the onion until it becomes translucent and edges begin to brown.
4. In a bowl, mix the spinach, sauteed onion, egg, egg whites, Parmesan cheese, 1/4 cup bread crumbs, salt, and pepper; set aside.
5. Spray sides and bottom of a 9-inch springform pan with nonstick cooking spray. Dust the entire inside of the pan with remaining 2 tablespoons bread crumbs.
6. Layer half of the potato slices in the bottom of the pan. Spread half the spinach mixture on top of the potatoes, followed by half the red peppers, half the mozzarella, and half the smoked meat. Repeat.
7. Top with a layer of potatoes arranged in overlapping circles. Brush potato layer lightly with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
8. Bake for 40 minutes. Remove from pan and cut into wedges. May be served warm or at room temperature. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
